key_insight: Automatic, independently chosen quadrature rules can break the expected superlinear convergence of policy iteration for Hamilton–Jacobi–Bellman equations; using matching quadrature restores it.
engineering_relevance: For finite element PDE solvers, this is a practical implementation warning: quadrature choices are not just a numerical detail and can materially affect convergence and reliability.
method: Compare policy iteration under automatic quadrature selection versus enforced matching quadrature.
Analyze the convergence behavior of finite element variational formulations for HJB equations.
Apply a simple quadrature-consistency fix to recover the theoretical convergence trend.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that nonmatching quadratures can cause loss of convergence, while matching quadrature recovers the expected superlinear convergence of policy iteration.
watch_item: The fix is implementation-sensitive: solver defaults that choose different quadratures for different variational terms may silently degrade convergence.
paper_title: The Effect of Quadrature on the Convergence of Policy Iteration for Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman Equations
